title: Golden Transcripts, net.Pipe, Differential Tests, Fuzzing, Race, and Benchmarks · Go Semantics Case: Differential Nethttp
summary: Tests send one complete request through loopback, compare only status, reuse the implementation as its own oracle, fuzz without invariants, and publish benchmark throughput without fragmentation or adversarial peers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the differential nethttp increment in layered deterministic protocol verification system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare supported inputs with Go net/http and independent recipients while treating deliberate strictness and implementation quirks as reviewed differences.
Use net.Pipe or a scripted short reader and writer to test fragmentation, blocking, deadlines, half-close, cancellation, and cleanup deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Differential Nethttp · Theory Differential Nethttp
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1463 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Differential Nethttp contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Defend the Differential Nethttp model · Theory Differential Nethttp
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Differential Nethttp: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory protocol team handles case 1469 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare supported inputs with Go net/http and independent recipients while treating deliberate strictness and implementation quirks as reviewed differences.
